recipes.title: Gochujang Sticky Beef Noodles With Charred Greens
recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Gochujang Sticky Beef Noodles With Charred Greens

Glossy fakeaway beef noodles with charred greens, adult garlic chilli oil, and a mild Louis bowl.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 38 minutes active | 42 minutes total

Yield: 3 bowls

Batch assumption: beef seared in 2 fast wok batches; Louis is split before adult sauce.

Meal role: weekend fakeaway sample

## Ingredients

### Protein

- Beef rump steak - 410g

### Carbs

- Egg noodles - 255g

### Vegetables

- Pak choi - 200g
- Tenderstem broccoli - 180g
- Red pepper - 145g
- Spring onions - 55g
- Garlic - 8g for garlic oil

### Sauce And Pantry

- Gochujang - 30g
- Soy sauce - 40ml
- Clear honey - 27g
- Rice vinegar - 15ml
- Sesame oil - 4ml
- Vegetable oil - 10ml
- Water - 80ml

### Finish

- Unsalted butter - 12g
- Sesame seeds - 8g
- Chilli flakes - 1 tsp (2g)

## Equipment

- Large carbon steel wok
- 20 cm Le Creuset tri-ply saucepan with lid
- Small 10 cm coated saucepan
- Mixing bowl
- Tongs
- Chopping board and knife

## Mise En Place

- Beef plate: slice the beef thinly against the grain.
- Charred veg bowl: halve the pak choi, halve thick broccoli stems, and cut the pepper into strips.
- Adult sauce bowl: mix the gochujang, 32ml soy sauce, 21g honey, 12ml rice vinegar, 3ml sesame oil, and 65ml water. Keep the butter beside the bowl.
- Louis mild glaze: mix 8ml soy sauce, 6g honey, 3ml rice vinegar, 1ml sesame oil, and 15ml water.
- Garlic oil setup: slice the garlic and keep the chilli flakes beside the small saucepan.
- Finish pot: slice the spring onions and keep them with the sesame seeds.

## Method

### 1. Cook the noodles

Cook the egg noodles together in the 20 cm saucepan. Drain and loosen with a little sesame oil.

Uses:

- Egg noodles - 255g
- Sesame oil - 1ml from the listed sauce oil

### 2. Mix the sauces

Keep the adult sauce and Louis glaze separate. Louis must stay away from gochujang and chilli oil.

Uses:

- Adult sauce bowl - all
- Louis mild glaze - all

### 3. Make the garlic chilli oil

Warm the small saucepan over low heat. Add vegetable oil and sliced garlic. Cook until lightly golden, add chilli flakes, then take the pan off the heat.

Uses:

- Vegetable oil - 10ml
- Garlic - 8g
- Chilli flakes - 1 tsp (2g)

### 4. Sear the beef

Heat the wok until very hot. Sear the beef in 2 fast batches so it browns instead of stewing. Move it to a plate.

Uses:

- Beef rump steak - 410g

### 5. Char the vegetables

Keep the wok hot. Add the whole charred veg bowl and let the edges catch colour before tossing.

Uses:

- Pak choi - 200g
- Tenderstem broccoli - 180g
- Red pepper - 145g

### 6. Split Louis's bowl

Remove Louis's share of noodles, beef, and charred veg. Toss it with his mild glaze.

Uses:

- Louis mild glaze - all
- Cooked egg noodles - Louis share
- Seared beef - Louis share
- Charred veg mix - Louis share

### 7. Glaze the adults

Return the adult beef and veg to the wok. Add the adult sauce and bubble until glossy. Stir in the butter, add the adult noodles, and toss hard.

Uses:

- Adult sauce - all
- Unsalted butter - 12g
- Cooked egg noodles - adult shares
- Seared beef - adult shares
- Charred veg mix - adult shares

### 8. Finish

Plate the bowls and finish the adult bowls with garlic chilli oil.

Uses:

- Spring onions - 55g
- Sesame seeds - 8g
- Garlic chilli oil - adult shares only

## Plate

### Luke

- Carbs: 150g dry-equivalent egg noodles, served as his share of the cooked noodle batch.
- Protein: about 44% of the seared beef batch.
- Vegetables: about 45% of the charred veg mix.
- Sauce: about 60% of the adult gochujang glaze.
- Finish: larger share of spring onions, sesame seeds, and garlic chilli oil.

### Sarah

- Carbs: 60g dry-equivalent egg noodles, served as her share of the cooked noodle batch.
- Protein: about 37% of the seared beef batch.
- Vegetables: about 37% of the charred veg mix.
- Sauce: about 40% of the adult gochujang glaze.
- Finish: smaller share of spring onions, sesame seeds, and garlic chilli oil.

### Louis

- Carbs: 45g dry-equivalent egg noodles, served as his share of the cooked noodle batch.
- Protein: about 19% of the seared beef batch.
- Vegetables: about 18% of the charred veg mix.
- Sauce: all of the mild honey-soy glaze.
- Finish: child share of spring onions and sesame seeds. No gochujang and no chilli oil.

## Nutrition And Cost

Sample estimate per serving:

- Luke: 1192 kcal, 72g protein, 149g carbs, 35g fat, GBP 4.80
- Sarah: 741 kcal, 51.5g protein, 74g carbs, 26.7g fat, GBP 3.73
- Louis: 417 kcal, 28.3g protein, 49.9g carbs, 10.7g fat, GBP 1.92

## Cook's Notes

- Sarah uses normal egg noodles because her saved issue is pasta-specific, not gluten-wide.
- Plate the cooked beef and veg by share of the cooked mix; do not separate the vegetables after stir-frying.
- Keep the wok hot so the beef browns quickly instead of stewing.

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttered Poached Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Soft poached eggs on hot buttered white sourdough, finished simply with Himalayan salt and black pepper.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 20 minutes

Yield: 3 plates

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 6 eggs
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 3 small knobs, 15g total

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 3 slices, about 180g total
- Himalayan salt - 3 pinches, about 0.9g total
- Black peppercorns - 3 light grinds, about 0.6g total

## Equipment

- 20 cm saucepan with lid
- Slotted spoon
- Oven grill or normal toaster route
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: set out 3 slices of white sourdough and 15g unsalted butter.
- Egg cups: set out 6 eggs; crack into cups if you want cleaner poaching.
- Finish station: keep the salt and pepper next to the plates.

## Method

### 1. Heat the water

Bring the saucepan of water to a gentle simmer. Lower the heat so the water is moving but not bubbling hard.

Uses:

- Water - enough for poaching

### 2. Toast and butter the sourdough

Toast the sourdough until golden and firm. Butter each slice while it is hot so the butter melts into the toast.

Uses:

- White sourdough bread - 3 slices
- Unsalted butter - 15g

### 3. Poach the eggs

Slide the eggs into the water. Cook gently until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft.

Uses:

- Eggs - 6

### 4. Plate and season

Lift the eggs out with a slotted spoon and drain briefly. Place 2 eggs onto each buttered toast slice, then season each plate.

Uses:

- Buttered sourdough toast - all
- Poached eggs - all
- Himalayan salt - 3 light pinches
- Black pepper - 3 light grinds

## Plate

### Luke

- 1 slice white sourdough toast, about 60g, buttered with 1 small knob of unsalted butter.
- 2 poached eggs.
- A light pinch of salt and a light grind of black pepper.

### Sarah

- 1 slice white sourdough toast, about 60g, buttered with 1 small knob of unsalted butter.
- 2 poached eggs.
- A light pinch of salt and a light grind of black pepper.

### Louis

- 1 slice white sourdough toast, about 60g, buttered with 1 small knob of unsalted butter.
- 2 poached eggs.
- A light pinch of salt and a light grind of black pepper.

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 333 kcal, 20.1g protein, 28.3g carbs, 16.3g fat, GBP 0.67
- Sarah: 333 kcal, 20.1g protein, 28.3g carbs, 16.3g fat, GBP 0.67
- Louis: 333 kcal, 20.1g protein, 28.3g carbs, 16.3g fat, GBP 0.67

## Cook's Notes

- Butter the toast as soon as it comes out, then put the eggs on top.
- If the pan feels crowded, poach the eggs in two rounds.
- Season after the eggs land so the toast stays crisp and the yolk carries the salt and pepper.

recipes.shopping_markdown:
# Buttered Poached Eggs On White Sourdough Toast - Recipe Shopping List

This is a recipe-level list grouped from `recipe-data-invoice.csv`, not a weekly shopping plan.

## Main Buy

### Dairy

- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - need 15g (3 small knob); pack 250g block; used cost GBP 0.12
- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - need 6 each (6 eggs); pack 15 egg pack; used cost GBP 1.17

## Pantry Check

### Pantry

- Black peppercorns - need 0.6g (3 light grind); pack 100g grinder; used cost GBP 0.00
- Himalayan salt - need 0.9g (3 pinch); pack 110g grinder; used cost GBP 0.03
- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - need 180g (3 slices); pack 500g loaf; used cost GBP 0.69

recipes.title: Sticky Soy Ginger Chicken Rice Bowls
recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Sticky Soy Ginger Chicken Rice Bowls

Generated from an existing V2 household recipe product.

Serves: Luke, Sarah, Louis

## Ingredients

### Aromatics

- Fresh ginger - 25.0g
- Garlic granules - 5.0g
- Soy sauce - 30.0ml

### Sauce And Pantry

- Clear honey - 25.0g
- Vegetable oil - 10.0ml

### Protein

- Chicken drum fillets - 450.0g

### Carbs

- Basmati rice - 225.0g

### Vegetables

- Frozen broccoli florets - 300.0g

## Equipment

- See the checked cook flow for pan and timing assumptions.

## Mise En Place

- Aromatics: set up the measured ingredients before cooking.
- Sauce And Pantry: set up the measured ingredients before cooking.
- Protein: set up the measured ingredients before cooking.
- Carbs: set up the measured ingredients before cooking.
- Vegetables: set up the measured ingredients before cooking.

## Method

### 1. Day 1 - Method

Bring the rice pan to a boil, cover, then simmer on low for 12 minutes.

### 2. Day 1 - Method

Take the rice off the heat and rest, covered, for 8 minutes before fluffing.

### 3. Day 1 - Method

Preheat a frying pan over medium-high heat for 3 minutes, then add 10ml vegetable oil.

### 4. Day 1 - Method

Cook the chicken for 8 minutes, turning until browned and 75C or no pink in the thickest piece.

### 5. Day 1 - Method

Pour the sauce into the pan and bubble for 2 minutes until glossy and coating the chicken.

### 6. Day 1 - Method

Boil the broccoli from frozen in a saucepan for 4 minutes, then drain well.

### 7. Day 1 - Mise en Place

Rinse 225g dry basmati rice in a sieve until the water runs mostly clear.

### 8. Day 1 - Mise en Place

Put the rice in a saucepan with 405ml water and a pinch of salt.

### 9. Day 1 - Mise en Place

Mix 30ml soy sauce, 25g honey, 25g grated ginger and 5g garlic granules in a bowl.

### 10. Day 1 - Mise en Place

Keep 300g frozen broccoli weighed and ready to boil near the end.

### 11. Day 1 - Mise en Place

Slice 450g raw chicken drum fillets into 2cm strips, then wash hands and keep the board separate.

### 12. Dish Up

Weigh cooked chicken: Luke 133g, Sarah 101g, Louis 117g.

### 13. Dish Up

Weigh cooked rice: Luke 280g, Sarah 210g, Louis 140g.

### 14. Dish Up

Weigh cooked broccoli: Luke 108g, Sarah 90g, Louis 72g.

### 15. Dish Up

Spoon any remaining pan sauce over the three bowls.

## Plate

### Luke

- Aromatics: Fresh ginger 10g; Soy sauce 12ml; Garlic granules 2g.
- Sauce And Pantry: Clear honey 11g; Vegetable oil 4ml.
- Protein: Chicken drum fillets 170g.
- Carbs: Basmati rice 100g.
- Vegetables: Frozen broccoli florets 120g.

### Sarah

- Aromatics: Fresh ginger 8g; Soy sauce 10ml; Garlic granules 2g.
- Sauce And Pantry: Clear honey 8g; Vegetable oil 3ml.
- Protein: Chicken drum fillets 130g.
- Carbs: Basmati rice 75g.
- Vegetables: Frozen broccoli florets 100g.

### Louis

- Aromatics: Fresh ginger 7g; Soy sauce 8ml; Garlic granules 1g.
- Sauce And Pantry: Clear honey 6g; Vegetable oil 3ml.
- Protein: Chicken drum fillets 150g.
- Carbs: Basmati rice 50g.
- Vegetables: Frozen broccoli florets 80g.

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 663.5 kcal, 49.7g protein, 99.8g carbs, 10.1g fat, GBP 1.33
- Sarah: 505.3 kcal, 38.4g protein, 75.4g carbs, 7.7g fat, GBP 1.04
- Louis: 422.3 kcal, 39.4g protein, 51.4g carbs, 8.0g fat, GBP 1.03

## Cook's Notes

- This V3 proof card is rendered from V2 source recipe rows. The app-facing invoice remains the source of truth for exact person-level data.
